explicit_text: I was born in The Bronx, New York, on July 14, 1933, and attended school there until the eighth grade. My family was part of a German-American community in The Bronx, which has since dispersed. Later in life, I also taught in Harlem, which is not far from The Bronx, so my life and work have been closely tied to New York City neighborhoods.

explicit_text: I participated in Freedom Summer in 1964 when I was 31 years old and already an experienced teacher. I was not a student then and made the decision independently, without consulting my parents. My involvement was motivated by my awareness of civil rights issues, especially after teaching African-American students in Harlem.

explicit_text: City College of New York was a beacon of opportunity for many students in Harlem and across the city because it offered tuition-free education for many years. I encouraged my students to consider City College as a path to a better life, though they often lacked role models who had succeeded there. The college's open enrollment policy allowed many to attend, but challenges remained for underprepared students.

entity: Board of Regents of the University of the State of New York
explicit_text: The Board of Regents of the University of the State of New York oversees education policies that affect institutions like City University. Currently, a Puerto Rican member of this board is leading efforts to cut back remedial programs, which is controversial among students. This ongoing struggle reflects the challenges in maintaining educational access and support for underprepared students.

entity: City University of New York Board of Regents
explicit_text: The City University of New York Board of Regents plays a critical role in shaping policies that impact tuition, enrollment, and remedial education. The board's decisions continue to influence the accessibility and quality of education for many New Yorkers.
